rationale: This pair is not an obvious incidental mention: the citing study’s core model is regulatory-focus fit in persuasion (viewer focus × message focus × frame), and it explicitly states it extends prior regulatory-focus/framing work. The cited 2006 JMR paper is a direct regulatory-focus fit paper with mechanism and boundary-condition evidence (selective processing, involvement), making it a plausible theory/mechanism source rather than a generic background citation.
